description: 10 Scientist Downgrade Dangers Os Fallout By Alan Emery WASHINGTON NANA A new report from governmen scientists sharply downgrades the dangers of radioactive fallout It claims that all radiation from fall out since the first atomic explo sions 15 years ago has had no more cumalaiive effect on an indi vidual than a routine chest xray and only a terth to a hundredth the effect of a fluoroscope picture The report by the Feaeral Radiation Council was submiited o the joini congressional commit tee on atomic energy Many scientists are now con vinced that last years estimates of dangers from radiation were exaggerated Atomic Energy Commission Chairman Glenn Seaborg says air pollution from autcnwobiles and factories provides a greater muen ace than radioactive tailout from nuclear tests s According to the council report the fallout from all nuclear iesis so far will only produce in present and future generations in the United States covering a 70year span about 110 dcformed babies 2000 cases of leukemia or blood cancer and 700 cases of hone can cer Over the same 70 years the Council estimates ap to 384000 persons will die from leukemia caused by natural radiation from the earth cosmic rays
